[David Kemps](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/david-kemps/index.html). How innovative are the companies in The Makers Top 100 based on their patents? And why is applying for patents important for manufacturing companies? “Trade secrets are only valuable if you really protect them,” says Marco Coolen, European patent attorney and partner at AOMB.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text:
How innovative are the companies in The Makers Top 100 based on their patents? And why is applying for patents important for manufacturing companies? “Trade secrets are only valuable if you really protect them,” says Marco Coolen, European patent attorney and partner at AOMB. 'A patent is often a silent force. It's frightening. But ultimately the judge determines how strong your patent really is.'. [Read also: Top 100 makers account for 102 billion turnover and 300,000 jobs](/nl/business/insights/sectors-and-trends/industry/makers-top-100-account-for-102-billion-turnover-and-300,000-jobs.html)
Manufacturing companies should check whether their IP is properly protected.

Manufacturing companies should check whether their IP is properly protected. Especially for Link Magazine, Marco Coolen and David Kemps from ABN AMRO analyzed the patent applications of companies from the first Makers Top 100.

Manufacturing companies should check whether their IP is properly protected. Especially for Link Magazine, Marco Coolen and David Kemps from ABN AMRO analyzed the patent applications of companies from the first Makers Top 100. "The positive news: 63 companies from the list have done something with patents in recent years," says Coolen. 'About fifteen companies submit applications on a regular basis, a group among them does so occasionally, and some have only one or a few applications to their name.'.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: Especially for Link Magazine, Marco Coolen and David Kemps from ABN AMRO analyzed the patent applications of companies from the first Makers Top 100. "The positive news: 63 companies from the list have done something with patents in recent years," says Coolen. 'About fifteen companies submit applications on a regular basis, a group among them does so occasionally, and some have only one or a few applications to their name.'. Companies that apply for patents often have their own products. The larger companies employ more R&D people, which result in more patents.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: "The positive news: 63 companies from the list have done something with patents in recent years," says Coolen. 'About fifteen companies submit applications on a regular basis, a group among them does so occasionally, and some have only one or a few applications to their name.'. Companies that apply for patents often have their own products. The larger companies employ more R&D people, which result in more patents. On average, in the Netherlands there are seven patent applications per hundred R&D people.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: 'About fifteen companies submit applications on a regular basis, a group among them does so occasionally, and some have only one or a few applications to their name.'. Companies that apply for patents often have their own products. The larger companies employ more R&D people, which result in more patents. On average, in the Netherlands there are seven patent applications per hundred R&D people. Not surprisingly, companies such as Philips, NXP, Signify and ASML top the list.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: Companies that apply for patents often have their own products. The larger companies employ more R&D people, which result in more patents. On average, in the Netherlands there are seven patent applications per hundred R&D people. Not surprisingly, companies such as Philips, NXP, Signify and ASML top the list. Names such as VDL and Lely also stand out. “These companies often employ their own patent attorneys because of the number of patent applications,” Coolen says.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: The larger companies employ more R&D people, which result in more patents. On average, in the Netherlands there are seven patent applications per hundred R&D people. Not surprisingly, companies such as Philips, NXP, Signify and ASML top the list. Names such as VDL and Lely also stand out. “These companies often employ their own patent attorneys because of the number of patent applications,” Coolen says. 'Patent attorneys make it easier to submit applications and use patents strategically.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text:
On average, in the Netherlands there are seven patent applications per hundred R&D people. Not surprisingly, companies such as Philips, NXP, Signify and ASML top the list. Names such as VDL and Lely also stand out. “These companies often employ their own patent attorneys because of the number of patent applications,” Coolen says. 'Patent attorneys make it easier to submit applications and use patents strategically. This in turn has a strengthening effect on the number of patent applications, causing the distance to the rest of the top 25 to grow rapidly.'
What does a patent attorney do and how long is a patent valid?

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text:
Not surprisingly, companies such as Philips, NXP, Signify and ASML top the list. Names such as VDL and Lely also stand out. “These companies often employ their own patent attorneys because of the number of patent applications,” Coolen says. 'Patent attorneys make it easier to submit applications and use patents strategically. This in turn has a strengthening effect on the number of patent applications, causing the distance to the rest of the top 25 to grow rapidly.'
What does a patent attorney do and how long is a patent valid? A patent attorney is legally authorized to apply for, file and process patents on behalf of companies or their clients – nationally and internationally.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text:
Names such as VDL and Lely also stand out. “These companies often employ their own patent attorneys because of the number of patent applications,” Coolen says. 'Patent attorneys make it easier to submit applications and use patents strategically. This in turn has a strengthening effect on the number of patent applications, causing the distance to the rest of the top 25 to grow rapidly.'
What does a patent attorney do and how long is a patent valid? A patent attorney is legally authorized to apply for, file and process patents on behalf of companies or their clients – nationally and internationally. He or she advises on how best to protect an invention, thinks strategically about the formulation of claims and supervises the entire application process.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text:
'Patent attorneys make it easier to submit applications and use patents strategically. This in turn has a strengthening effect on the number of patent applications, causing the distance to the rest of the top 25 to grow rapidly.'
What does a patent attorney do and how long is a patent valid? A patent attorney is legally authorized to apply for, file and process patents on behalf of companies or their clients – nationally and internationally. He or she advises on how best to protect an invention, thinks strategically about the formulation of claims and supervises the entire application process. A standard patent is valid for twenty years from the date of filing.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text:
This in turn has a strengthening effect on the number of patent applications, causing the distance to the rest of the top 25 to grow rapidly.'
What does a patent attorney do and how long is a patent valid? A patent attorney is legally authorized to apply for, file and process patents on behalf of companies or their clients – nationally and internationally. He or she advises on how best to protect an invention, thinks strategically about the formulation of claims and supervises the entire application process. A standard patent is valid for twenty years from the date of filing. The condition is that maintenance costs are paid annually.

A standard patent is valid for twenty years from the date of filing. The condition is that maintenance costs are paid annually. Keep competition at bay. metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: A standard patent is valid for twenty years from the date of filing. The condition is that maintenance costs are paid annually. Keep competition at bay. Why are patents important for manufacturing companies? “Patents can protect an innovation for twenty years,” Coolen explains. 'This allows you to keep competitors at bay or generate income by issuing licenses.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: The condition is that maintenance costs are paid annually. Keep competition at bay. Why are patents important for manufacturing companies? “Patents can protect an innovation for twenty years,” Coolen explains. 'This allows you to keep competitors at bay or generate income by issuing licenses. Companies such as Philips and Signify have a lot of experience with licenses.' Coolen therefore advocates that manufacturing companies check for themselves whether their IP is well protected.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: Keep competition at bay. Why are patents important for manufacturing companies? “Patents can protect an innovation for twenty years,” Coolen explains. 'This allows you to keep competitors at bay or generate income by issuing licenses. Companies such as Philips and Signify have a lot of experience with licenses.' Coolen therefore advocates that manufacturing companies check for themselves whether their IP is well protected. According to Kemps, patents are also valuable for investors.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: Why are patents important for manufacturing companies? “Patents can protect an innovation for twenty years,” Coolen explains. 'This allows you to keep competitors at bay or generate income by issuing licenses. Companies such as Philips and Signify have a lot of experience with licenses.' Coolen therefore advocates that manufacturing companies check for themselves whether their IP is well protected. According to Kemps, patents are also valuable for investors. 'They provide legal certainty about the uniqueness of products or techniques.

'This allows you to keep competitors at bay or generate income by issuing licenses. Companies such as Philips and Signify have a lot of experience with licenses.' Coolen therefore advocates that manufacturing companies check for themselves whether their IP is well protected. According to Kemps, patents are also valuable for investors. Have the application drawn up and checked by a patent attorney. Pay close attention to the conclusions (claims) – they determine the scope of your protection. Only make your invention/innovation public after submission

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: Kemps does make an important comment. 'It is not the case that patents are attractive for all innovations. Manufacturing companies without their own products also innovate, for example in internal processes. Keeping these innovations secret is often more attractive. With a patent you publish your innovation.

metadata.source_title: Keeping competitors at bay with patents

text: 'It is not the case that patents are attractive for all innovations. Manufacturing companies without their own products also innovate, for example in internal processes. Keeping these innovations secret is often more attractive. With a patent you publish your innovation. Your competitor can then build on this.' Still, the research into the Makers Top 100 offers interesting insights, according to Kemps.
